title: Percentage Change Mistakes That Skew Business Reports
summary: Month-over-month percent change is simple until divides-by-zero, small bases, and calendar effects appear.

content.summary: Month-over-month percent change is simple until divides-by-zero, small bases, and calendar effects appear.

body.text: A 200% jump from one to three units is mathematically true and practically meaningless. Dividing by last period when it was zero or near-zero creates absurd percentages. Partial months compare unfairly to full months. Mixing filtered subsets—this month all regions, last month one region—distorts deltas.

body.alt: Data visualization on a modern display
body.caption: Data visualization on a modern display. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Dividing by last period when it was zero or near-zero creates absurd percentages. Partial months compare unfairly to full months.
Mixing filtered subsets—this month all regions, last month one region—distorts deltas.

items: Require minimum base volume before showing percent change.

body.text: Use saved rules for consistent scope both periods. Chart absolute values when bases are tiny.
Finance partners appreciate absolute numbers with context over flashy percentages.
Datilio filters and side-by-side period charts reduce percent-change errors.
